ch: 我的爸爸是部落的獵人。
en: My dad is a hunter of the tribe.

ab: yako ray ’ima ’ol’olae’an, yaba’ ki mama’ ray ’ima rohaenan rima’ ’omalep, maraS ka sibal ki wawatos.
ch: 我小的時候，爸爸和叔叔會在晚上出門，帶著獵刀和獵槍。
en: When I was little, my dad and uncle would go out at night with hunting knives and guns.

ab: ’ima Sera: ila, lasiya’ malibih ka ’inalep kakrangi’an lobih ray taew’an.
ch: 天亮時，他們就會帶著獵到的獵物回來。
en: At daybreak, they returned with their prey.

ab: mak’aehae’ ma’ waliSan, mak’aehae’ ma’ hapis, ’oka’ ma’ kaehka:.
ch: 有時候是山豬、有時候是飛鼠，偶爾也會有雉雞。
en: Sometimes it was a boar, sometimes a flying squirrel, and occasionally a pheasant.

ch: 他們會在村口將獵物分好，分享給族人。
en: They would divide the prey at the entrance of the village and share it with the tribe.

ab: yako rengreng ’omaybel hi yaba’, ’am taniSowaw hi yaba’ rima’ ’omalep.
ch: 我每次都會吵著爸爸，要跟著爸爸去打獵。
en: Every time I quarreled with my father, I wanted to go hunting with my father.

ab: yaba’ Sa’ So: ya:o ’ibabaw ila, siya ’am maraS ila ’iyakin.
ch: 爸爸說等我長大了他再帶我一起去。
en: Dad said that he would take me with me when I grew up.

ab: ray ’asang kamanra:an noka baseng romakep ka kakrangi’an, lasiya ma’ ra:am Sitalnga’ ki linanger maSkan ka binaseng.
ch: 部落的男人也會用陷阱抓獵物，他們會製作套索陷阱和石壓式陷阱。
en: The men of the tribe also trap their prey, making lasso traps and rock traps.

ab: ma’an yaba’ ra:am pakkayzaeh ka talnga’, yaba’ mayaka:i’ Sa’, hini nom romakep ka kaehka:, ’ita’ pakkayzaeh kita’ noka ka’alepen ka Sinarakan, baseng ’aSkan ray kapayziza’an nasiya’.
ch: 我的爸爸很會製作套索陷阱，爸爸說，這是用來捕抓雉雞的，我們要仔細觀察獵物的腳印，陷阱要放在獵物走的路上。
en: My dad is very good at making lasso traps. Dad said that this is used to catch pheasants. We have to carefully observe the footprints of the prey, and the traps should be placed on the path of the prey.

ab: ’am ’omam’amoeh ma’onhael kil tatini’ rima’ ’omalep, yako sa’sa’ih ka hahila: lomoehloehay paskayzaeh nom baseng, kin’alikaeh ma’an ’inaSkan ka baseng, pateleng ila ka ’inalep.
ch: 為了可以趕快和大人一起去打獵，我每天練習製作陷阱，很快地我的陷阱就真的可以抓到獵物了。
en: In order to go hunting with adults as soon as possible, I practiced making traps every day, and soon my traps can really catch prey.

ab: yaba’ komita’ manabih ’iyakin, mayhael So’o ’am ’ima wa’isan kama’alep.
ch: 爸爸看到後對我說我以後一定會是很厲害的獵人。
en: Dad saw it and told me that I will be a very good hunter in the future.

ab: ’isahini yako ’ibabaw ila, ya:o pama’ ka boehoe: ki siwa:, tani hi yaba’ rima’ ’omalep.
ch: 現在我長大了，我背起我的弓和箭矢，跟著爸爸一起去打獵。
en: Now that I have grown up, I carry my bow and arrows and go hunting with my father.

ch: 回村時將獵到的獵物和族人們分享。
en: When returning to the village, share the hunted prey with the tribe.

ab: yaba’ Sa’, ma’alo’ a tomal noka tatini’ ka hino’alo’, mobe:ay ’ini mita’ kin’akoey ka ’inalep.
ch: 爸爸說，我們要感謝祖靈賜福，讓我們有很多獵物收穫。
en: Dad said, we want to thank the ancestors for their blessings, so that we have a lot of prey to harvest.
